passage: Mortality dropped 40% and readmissions fell 25% (Vega, 2023).
source_excerpt: All-cause mortality declined by 15% versus control. Readmission outcomes were not reported.

sourceQuotes: All-cause mortality declined by 15%
rationale: Passage cites 40% but excerpt reports 15%
Readmission outcomes were not reported in the excerpt

passage: The program cut costs by 30% and doubled enrollment (Ruiz, 2022).

sourceQuotes: Operating costs fell by 8%
rationale: Passage cites 30% but excerpt reports 8%
Enrollment was outside the scope of this analysis

passage: Response rates reached 80% and toxicity was negligible (Cho, 2021).

sourceQuotes: Objective response was observed in 52%
rationale: Passage cites 80% but excerpt reports 52%
Toxicity data were not included in this excerpt

passage: HbA1c fell 2.0 points and quality of life doubled (Nash, 2020).

rationale: Passage cites 2.0-point drop but excerpt reports 0.9
Patient-reported outcomes were not assessed

passage: Wait times were halved and patient satisfaction rose 50% (Marsh, 2019).

Satisfaction surveys were not conducted

passage: Relapse fell 55% and remission lasted two years (Aydin, 2024).

sourceQuotes: relapse risk decreased by 19%
rationale: Passage cites 55% but excerpt reports 19%
Long-term remission was not evaluated in the excerpt

passage: Infection rates dropped 70% and antibiotic use ceased (Hall, 2022).

rationale: Passage cites 70% but excerpt reports 22%

passage: Graduation rates rose 35% and dropout disappeared (Vega, 2021).

passage: Pain scores improved 40% and function fully recovered (Owens, 2023).

claims.claim: Pain relief was equal across arms

rationale: Excerpt hedges with may appear comparable; passage states equal relief
Billing analyses were beyond the scope of this report
